---
title: "Getting Started"
description: "Install Godot, import the project, and run your first battle."
---

Sparta is a **Godot 4.6** project. It needs no environment variables, no build
step, and --- crucially --- **no downloaded art**: units draw themselves as colored
placeholder tokens, so the battle is playable the moment you press play.

## 1. Install Godot 4.6 (Standard build)

Download **Godot 4.6.x --- Standard build** (GDScript, *not* the .NET/C# build) from
the [official downloads page](https://godotengine.org/download/).

::: {.callout-important}
Use the **Standard** build, not **.NET**. Sparta is pure GDScript; the .NET build
is for C# projects and is not what this project targets.
:::

## 2. Import the project

1. Open Godot.
2. Click **Import**.
3. Select this repository's `project.godot`.

## 3. Play

Press **F5** (Play). The battle starts immediately:

- Two armies spawn --- [**blue**]{.army-blue} at the top (yours),
  [**red**]{.army-red} at the bottom (the enemy).
- Units are colored tokens on a green field. No art download is required.

That's it --- you're in a battle. Head to **[How to Play](how-to-play.qmd)** for the
controls, or **[Tactics That Matter](tactics.qmd)** to start winning.

## Running headlessly (optional)

You don't need a display to *validate* the project --- handy for CI or a cloud
editor. From the project root:

```bash
# Catch parse/load errors without opening the editor:
godot --headless --path . --quit
```

To actually *play*, though, you need the desktop editor with a display --- a headless
environment has no window to render the battle into.

### Installing Godot in a headless Linux environment

Most Linux CI (and remote/cloud editors) can run Godot headlessly once it's
installed. Add this to a setup script (matches the project's Godot version):

```bash
wget -q https://github.com/godotengine/godot/releases/download/4.6-stable/Godot_v4.6-stable_linux.x86_64.zip -O /tmp/godot.zip
mkdir -p "$HOME/.local/bin/"
unzip -q /tmp/godot.zip -d "$HOME/.local/bin/"
mv "$HOME/.local/bin/Godot_v4.6-stable_linux.x86_64" "$HOME/.local/bin/godot"
chmod +x "$HOME/.local/bin/godot"
rm /tmp/godot.zip
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
```

::: {.callout-note}
If the engine version changes, update **both** the download URL and the binary
filename in the snippet above.
:::

## Tests

The project has a GUT test suite (vendored at run time in CI). See
[`test/`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/tree/main/test) in the repository
for how it's wired.

---

*Source: [`README.md`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/README.md)
in the repository.*
